verses.text: The fathers fear the daughters' inheritance will pass to another tribe. The concern. Land might be lost.

God rules the heiresses must marry within their own tribe. The rule. Inheritance stays in the tribe.

God rules the heiresses must marry within their own tribe. The rule. Inheritance stays in the tribe.
So no inheritance moves from tribe to tribe. Kept. Each tribe holds its land.
So no inheritance moves from tribe to tribe. Kept. Each tribe holds its land.
The daughters obey and marry their cousins. Obeyed. They keep God's command.

These are the commandments given in the plains of Moab. The close. The book ends at Jordan's edge.
